title: Container not accessible outside host due to lack of forwarding
body:
This is on a Fedora 31 system.  I'm brand new to podman, using it because of the cgroups2 issue with docker -- which is fine by me, since I am pretty new to docker to start with.

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

After dealing with issue 4114: https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/4114
By running `ip link add cni-podman0 type bridge`, a rootfull container started thus:

    podman run -p 80:80/tcp ....


Has port 80 accessible from the host but not beyond that (icmp type 3 "unreachable"); the server is bound to 0.0.0.0 in the container:
```
host> netstat -lnp
Proto Recv-Q Send-Q Local Address           Foreign Address         State       PID/Program name    
tcp        0      0 0.0.0.0:80              0.0.0.0:*               LISTEN      -                   

host> iptables -L -v
Chain INPUT (policy ACCEPT 329 packets, 47703 bytes)
 pkts bytes target     prot opt in     out     source               destination         

Chain FORWARD (policy DROP 0 packets, 0 bytes)
 pkts bytes target     prot opt in     out     source               destination         
   49  6489 CNI-FORWARD  all  --  any    any     anywhere             anywhere             /* CNI firewall plugin rules */
    4   240 REJECT     all  --  any    any     anywhere             anywhere             reject-with icmp-port-unreachable

Chain OUTPUT (policy ACCEPT 335 packets, 36724 bytes)
 pkts bytes target     prot opt in     out     source               destination         

Chain CNI-ADMIN (1 references)
 pkts bytes target     prot opt in     out     source               destination         

Chain CNI-FORWARD (1 references)
 pkts bytes target     prot opt in     out     source               destination         
   49  6489 CNI-ADMIN  all  --  any    any     anywhere             anywhere             /* CNI firewall plugin rules */
```

I'm not all that proficient with these things, but the empty CNI-ADMIN chain referenced by the CNI-FORWARD chain looks responsible, so I tried:

    iptables -A CNI-ADMIN -j ACCEPT

And this works, I dunno how robust or appropriate a solution it really is -- right now I only need the one container, I think it should do for that.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

Happens every time the system is rebooted; starting a container fails immediately because of issue 4114, once that is corrected the container will start but be unaccessible beyond the host until that accept rule is added to the CNI-ADMIN chain.

**Describe the results you expected:**

I'm hoping to have this stuff automated properly for me :)

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.6.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.1
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:                                
  compiler: gc                                   
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.13.1      
  podman version: 1.6.2                       
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.11.3
  CgroupVersion: v2
  Conmon:
    package: conmon-2.0.2-1.fc31.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.2, commit: 186a550ba0866ce799d74006dab97969a2107979'
  Distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    version: "31"
  MemFree: 5053612032
  MemTotal: 11988262912
  OCIRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: crun-0.10.6-1.fc31.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 0.10.6
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  SwapFree: 16683962368
  SwapTotal: 17365110784
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 4
  eventlogger: journald
  hostname: localhost.localdomain
  kernel: 5.4.0-i5Asus
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 49h 52m 14.95s (Approximately 2.04 days)
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org
  - quay.io
store:
  ConfigFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 3
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
    overlay.mountopt: nodev,metacopy=on
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "true"
  ImageStore:
    number: 9
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  VolumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-1.6.2-2.fc31.x86_64
```

Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):

I'm working via `ssh root@localhost` because of the issue with `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR`: https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1776187 
(Can't use `su root` because "podman on cgroup V2 needs to have a full systemd login session to work".)

comments.body: @dcbw any idea what might be going on here/
Can you paste how are you testing the connection to the port 80 of the host?

Sorry for the delay gang.

How would you like me to test it?

We're stepping outside my area of expertise here, but `telnet` and `netstat` to see if the port is open on the host and remote, plus the output of `iptables -nvL` and `iptables -t nat -nvL` would help us diagnose.

I suspect from this we're going to have some missing IPTables forwarding rules, given that CNI was unable to even create a bridge for you; there's definitely something going wrong on their end, and it doesn't seem to be generating errors.

(A `podman run --log-level=debug` trace of your Podman command would help here, maybe CNI is generating warnings that aren't being printed)
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@MK27 Are you still interested in this?  You never gave @mheon the output of the podman run --log-level=debug?
Closing for lack of response.  Reopen if this is still an issue for you.
